name: Flow DSL
description: Ship the public flow-authoring surface: defineFlow, the five step builders, and the DAG/validation plumbing that turns a FlowSpec into an executable Flow. By the end of this sprint a user can write a flow.ts file that compiles and whose graph validates — even though no runner exists yet to execute it.

waves.name: Flow and Step core types
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/flow/types.ts` with the shared type declarations used by defineFlow and all step builders. Types: `StepBase` (id added by flow compiler), `StepKind = 'prompt'|'script'|'branch'|'parallel'|'terminal'`, `PromptStepSpec`, `PromptStepOutput` (discriminated union of `{handoff, schema?}`, `{artifact}`, `{handoff, artifact, schema?}`), `ScriptStepSpec`, `BranchStepSpec` (Omit<ScriptStepSpec,'output'> & {onExit: required}), `ParallelStepSpec`, `TerminalStepSpec`, `Step` (discriminated union on kind), `FlowSpec<TInput>` (name, version, description?, defaultProvider?, input: ZodSchema, steps: Record<string,Step>, start?: string), `Flow<TInput>` (FlowSpec plus compiled `graph: FlowGraph`, `stepOrder: string[]`, `rootSteps: string[]`). Also types for `RunState`, `StepState` per §4.8.1 (add here so they are available to later sprints). All TypeScript-only; no runtime logic.

waves.name: step.prompt builder
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/flow/steps/prompt.ts` exporting `promptStep(spec: PromptStepSpec): Step` (to be wired into the `step.prompt` namespace). Responsibilities: validate the spec at build time (throw `FlowDefinitionError` if `promptFile` missing, if `output` is not one of the three allowed shapes, if `maxRetries < 0`, if both `handoff` schema and non-Zod value provided). Default values: `maxRetries: 0`, `timeoutMs: 600_000`, `onFail: 'abort'`. Return a normalized Step object tagged `kind: 'prompt'`. Do not execute anything. Reference §4.4.1.

waves.name: step.script builder
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/flow/steps/script.ts` exporting `scriptStep(spec: ScriptStepSpec): Step`. Validate at build time: `run` required (string OR non-empty string array), `onExit` keys are numeric-string or 'default'. If `run` is a string, document that it will be shlex-split at runtime (the builder does not split). Defaults: timeoutMs undefined (no timeout unless provided), onFail 'abort'. Return a Step tagged `kind: 'script'`. Reference §4.4.2.

waves.name: step.branch builder
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/flow/steps/branch.ts` exporting `branchStep(spec: BranchStepSpec): Step`. Same validation as script, but `onExit` is REQUIRED (throw FlowDefinitionError if missing or empty). No `output` allowed (enforce at type + runtime). Return a Step tagged `kind: 'branch'`. Reference §4.4.3.

waves.name: step.parallel builder
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/flow/steps/parallel.ts` exporting `parallelStep(spec: ParallelStepSpec): Step`. Validate: `branches` is a non-empty string array. No further validation here (existence of branch IDs is checked at flow compile time in task_17). Defaults: onFail 'abort'. Return a Step tagged `kind: 'parallel'`. Reference §4.4.4.

waves.name: step.terminal builder
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/flow/steps/terminal.ts` exporting `terminalStep(spec: TerminalStepSpec): Step`. Trivial — just normalize defaults (`exitCode: 0`), return Step tagged `kind: 'terminal'`. Reference §4.4.5.

waves.name: DAG builder with cycle detection and topological sort
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/flow/graph.ts` exporting `buildGraph(steps: Record<string,Step>, start?: string): FlowGraph`. The graph holds: forward edges (stepId -> string[] dependents), reverse edges (stepId -> dependsOn[]), and a precomputed topological order. Implementation: Kahn's algorithm for topo sort. On cycle, throw `FlowDefinitionError` with the cycle printed. Also validate: (a) every `dependsOn` references an existing step, (b) every `branches:[]` in a parallel step references an existing step, (c) every `onFail: <stepId>` string target exists, (d) every `onExit` value that isn't 'abort'/'continue' exists, (e) every referenced handoff in `contextFrom` is written by an upstream step. Also compute `rootSteps` (no deps) and resolve the effective entry: if `start` given, use it; else require exactly one root or throw FlowDefinitionError suggesting `start:`. Export also `FlowGraph` type.

waves.name: defineFlow and step namespace wiring
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/flow/define.ts` exporting `defineFlow<TInput>(spec: FlowSpec<TInput>): Flow<TInput>`. Implementation: assign step IDs from the record keys, call each builder to normalize, run `buildGraph` (task_17), populate `Flow.graph`, `stepOrder`, `rootSteps`, and return the sealed Flow object. Also create `packages/core/src/flow/step.ts` that exports a `step` namespace object { prompt, script, branch, parallel, terminal } each bound to the respective builder from wave 2. Re-export `defineFlow`, `step`, and `Flow`, `FlowSpec`, all Step spec types from `src/index.ts` per §4.2. Validate flow-level fields: name required (kebab-case), version required (semver-ish regex), input is a Zod schema. Throw FlowDefinitionError on any failure.
